generated_comment: Bro people need to chill a bit about gaalis. In college, gaming, office banter, everyone uses those mother-sister type words as filler only, not literally about women. Same with saying don't act like a girl when someone panics yaar, it's just expression, not some social crime.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body and female-relationship-based abuse as harmless slang and dismisses criticism with the 'it's just expression' defense. It also encodes feminizing-as-insult through 'don't act like a girl' as a naturalized part of male speech.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 27. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are posting anonymously on Reddit after your male colleague told you that his girlfriend of three years cheated on him with their mutual friend. You are writing a comment about what this says about women in general and whether men can ever fully trust women in relationships.
